title: Tenure lifecycle P1: three new stages + plum border system pattern
summary: Shipped Stage 07b (performance review cycle), Stage 08b (defensive-AI review panel for Ember's draft), and Stage 08c (return from leave re-onboarding) in correct scroll order between existing stages. Applied plum 3px left border to all eight human-required rows system-wide as a permanent pattern. P1-2 contrast claim investigated: existing cost panels already use ochre-link (#7a3d0a, 7.14:1 AAA) and paper-rule for agent names — no rework required; findings documented.

frames_created.name: Tenure / Stage 07b · Year 1 · Performance review cycle
Tenure / Stage 08b · Aisha reviews Ember's draft

meta.p1_2_finding: No cost-panel contrast failure found. Agent names use ochre-link (#7a3d0a) on ink (#0e1626) = 7.14:1 AAA. The ochre (#b8651f) instances are decorative bullet markers on paper (3.63:1, SC 1.4.11 non-text threshold = 3:1, passing). Cost-panel rework not required.
meta.lifecycle_dots: Kept at 10 dots. Stages 07b, 08b, 08c designated as between-stage frames with no dot increment. Eyebrow pattern: 'STAGE 07b · BETWEEN 07 AND 08'.
meta.cost_rollup: Three new frames add $0.078 total ($0.038 + $0.012 + $0.028). Under $0.05 per frame; five-year total footnoted but Lifecycle summary and Agent allocation frames not rebuilt.
meta.o1_note: Dispatch closes the ochre-on-ink misidentification (contrast was already correct). Plum border pattern advances O-1 by making human-required rows visually distinct without color-only differentiation — the bullet icon and 'Human action required' text remain as non-color cues.
